summary: A comprehensive academic discussion section synthesizing the spatial, descriptive, and inferential statistical findings from the Shama Municipality water access study. It connects the empirical results (such as the weak direct correlation between water distance/WAI and income, and the highly significant association between water source type and economic impact) to the DFID Sustainable Livelihoods Framework. The discussion highlights the mediated pathways of impact (time-poverty and health burden) and the critical spatial disparities observed across communities.
